data.narrative:
AnySignal is a Series A–stage space and aerospace company founded by John Malsbury, Ricky Medina, and Jeffrey R Osborne. The company has raised $34M+ to date. The valuation universe includes five public comparables (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and eight private peers including SpaceX, Sierra Space, and Planet Labs, though direct same-stage and same-tier filtering yielded insufficient datapoints for robust peer-median analysis.

Only one valuation method proved applicable given available data. Stage-progression analysis uses the $34M total raised as a proxy baseline and applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A progression to subsequent rounds, implying a valuation of $68M. Public-comp anchoring cannot be executed without disclosed revenue; the Space & Aerospace sector median trades at 25.8x PS across six public tickers, but this multiple cannot be applied to an undisclosed revenue base. Peer-median comparison was rejected due to insufficient same-stage and adjacent-stage private comparables within narrow valuation bands.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $58M to $78M, with a median point of $68M. The narrow methodological foundation—reliance on a single applicable approach—represents the primary uncertainty. Revenue disclosure or comparable transaction precedent within the narrow Series A space-tech cohort would materially strengthen conviction in this range.
